atomicity.message: The staged diff is exactly one Unit E feature slice: two new files, internal/backpressure/lint_detect.go and internal/backpressure/lint_detect_test.go, adding bounded Go and Node/Astro lint detection facts/candidate commands plus focused tests. No CLI wizard, manifest writer, config storage, CI parsing, or unrelated runtime changes are staged.

conditions.message: The lint-detection rules are bounded and command-backed: Go candidates are standard Go commands only when go is available, Node candidates come only from exact existing allowed scripts, and generated/vendor directories are excluded.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status showed only A internal/backpressure/lint_detect.go and A internal/backpressure/lint_detect_test.go for this staged slice.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_detect.go found nodeLintScriptNames limited to lint, test, typecheck, check, build, format:check; Go commands are go test ./..., go vet ./..., and gofmt check, proposed only when GoAvailable is true.

git show :internal/backpressure/lint_detect_test.go found tests for single Go root, exact Node scripts, polyglot, multi-node roots, excluded node_modules/vendor/dist/docs/demos/generated, missing Go, and package-manager lockfile selection.
Temporary staged snapshot focused lint-detect tests passed.

conditions.message: The staged implementation factors repeated decisions into small helpers instead of duplicating detection logic across Go, Node, command generation, path normalization, and tests.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected separate helpers DetectLintSetup, shouldSkipLintDetectionDir, detectNodeRoot, readLintPackageScripts, detectNodePackageManager, lintDetectionIsMultiRoot, lintDetectionCandidateCommands, lintCandidateCommand, nodeRunCommand, lintCommandIDPart, and normalizeDetectedRootPath.
BrightOwl found shared test helpers for tool lookup and root/command extraction rather than repeated extraction code in each test.
git diff --cached --check passed.

conditions.message: The implementation does not inflate lint coverage claims: it reports detection facts and candidate commands only from bounded first-increment evidence, and missing Go keeps facts without proposing non-runnable Go commands.
evidence: BrightOwl checked the bead scope: bounded Go/Node detection, no CI parsing, no other languages, and no enforcement claim unless a command/analyzer runs.
BrightOwl verified CandidateCommands are derived only from detected Go roots with GoAvailable or exact Node package scripts; missing Go does not generate Go commands.
BrightOwl verified tests include missing-Go and exact-script assertions, including rejecting format when only format:check is allowed.

conditions.message: The public detection behavior and important helper branches are covered by focused tests for every behavior named in the bead.
evidence: BrightOwl verified tests for single Go root, single Node root exact scripts, Go+Node polyglot, multiple Node packages, excluded generated/vendor dirs, missing Go, and package manager lockfile selection.
The staged tests cover exact-script filtering, excluded directories, multi-root heuristic, missing Go path, and each lockfile manager case named in the bead.
Temporary staged snapshot focused lint-detect tests passed.

conditions.message: The staged slice meets the Unit E done criteria: it adds non-mutating Go/Node detection, candidate command generation, exact-script and lockfile behavior, exclusions, multi-root flags, and focused tests; full tests pass in a staged snapshot.
evidence: BrightOwl checked br show project-readiness-kit-lint-unit-e-detection-l7k --json against the implementation.
BrightOwl verified exported LintDetection/LintGoRoot/LintNodeRoot facts and DetectLintSetup candidate command generation.
Temporary staged snapshot focused lint-detect tests passed.
Temporary staged snapshot go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The packet and staged code provide traceable evidence for the detector behavior, including staged payload hash, manifest hash, condition hashes, staged path details, and tests for the stated cases.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified staged_payload_hash sha256:4d6481532944b05ffdd58a7f75ba303507ee11523d825d7e0a3072f4c10f47cc and manifest_hash sha256:00e2a9854cb8dcbdde3e942841f405f13b196fd8b3ebe884380dd9f8c0fc8df5 from the packet JSON.
Packet staged_payload_details lists both lint detection files as hash_included=true and generated=false.
ScarletMarsh ran focused tests and go test ./... in a clean index snapshot; direct worktree tests were not used because unrelated dirty cmd/burpvalve/main.go had build errors.

conditions.message: The staged payload stays inside Unit E's bounded detection scope and does not implement deferred wizard flow, CI parsing, other languages, config storage, or file mutation.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status listed only the two new internal/backpressure lint detection files.
BrightOwl noted unrelated worktree changes were unstaged and not part of this packet's staged payload.
BrightOwl verified no CLI/wizard/config files are staged and no CI parser or Python/Rust detection is present.

conditions.message: The detector reads repo files and proposes commands as data; it does not execute candidate commands, write files, delete files, reset git state, send network requests, or perform external mutations.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected staged code and found filepath.WalkDir, os.ReadFile, os.Stat, and exec.LookPath; no exec.Command, WriteFile, Remove/RemoveAll, git reset/clean, rm -rf, or network URL additions are present.
DetectLintSetup only builds LintDetection and CandidateCommands; candidate commands are strings inside lintconfig.Command, not executed by this code.
Tests write only to t.TempDir fixtures through existing test helpers.

conditions.message: The detection facts are deterministic and preserve relevant data: roots are normalized/sorted, package manager and lockfile are retained, exact scripts are retained in allowed order, and command IDs are sanitized.
evidence: BrightOwl verified filepath.Abs/defaultRoot, filepath.WalkDir, filepath.Rel plus filepath.ToSlash normalization, sorting of GoRoots and NodeRoots, and normalized root paths using '.'.
BrightOwl verified detectNodePackageManager returns both manager and lockfile and lintCommandIDPart lowercases/sanitizes command ID components.
BrightOwl verified tests assert root paths, package manager lockfile selection, exact script list order, node root ordering, and generated/vendor exclusion.

conditions.message: The governed security surface was inspected. The code reads package metadata and lockfile presence but does not execute discovered scripts, mutate project files, read environment secrets, or cross into auth/tenant/PII behavior.
evidence: BrightOwl's mutation/execution API scan found os.ReadFile and os.Stat plus command-string construction; no os.WriteFile/Create/Mkdir/Remove/Rename/Chmod/OpenFile or exec.Command Run/Start/Output calls are present.
BrightOwl verified LookPath only checks Go availability, while Node scripts are parsed as json.RawMessage keys and converted to candidate command strings, not run.
Temporary staged snapshot go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload has no governed visual surface: it adds backend lint-detection logic and tests, not UI, screenshots, responsive layout, canvas, or visual-diff behavior.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status lists only internal/backpressure/lint_detect.go and internal/backpressure/lint_detect_test.go as new files.
backpressure/visual-regression.md governs screenshot, browser, responsive layout, canvas, and visual-diff checks; none of those surfaces are changed by this staged payload.

conditions.message: The detector does a single repo WalkDir, skips known heavy/generated directories, reads only go.mod/package.json/lockfile metadata, and produces bounded candidate command slices. No unbounded command execution, network work, or concurrency fanout is introduced.
evidence: DetectLintSetup uses filepath.WalkDir once over the root and shouldSkipLintDetectionDir skips .git, node_modules, vendor, dist, and docs/demos/generated, including nested prefixes.
The detector reads package.json files only when encountered, checks local lockfile names with os.Stat, sorts discovered roots, and appends a small fixed set of candidate commands per root/script.
Clean index snapshot passed go test ./...; focused detection tests include excluded generated/vendor dirs and multi-root behavior.

conditions.message: The staged detector stays within autonomous discovery: it returns facts and candidate lint commands, marks multi-root repos as needing scoped setup, and does not apply config, install dependencies, or mutate project files without a later user/setup step.
evidence: LintDetection includes GoRoots, NodeRoots, MultiRoot, NeedsScopedSetup, and CandidateCommands as data returned to callers.

lintDetectionIsMultiRoot sets NeedsScopedSetup for Go+Node or multiple roots, matching the approved direction that scoped lint setup becomes a first-class question rather than silently applying global commands.
No staged code writes backpressure/manifest.yaml, project config, package manager files, or hooks; command generation is advisory data only.
